---
params:
  year: 2026
  month: 1
  municipality_codes: [
    2507507, # João Pessoa (PB)
    3106200, # Belo Horizonte (MG)
    3550308, # São Paulo (SP)
    4314902, # Porto Alegre (RS)
    1501402 # Belém (PA)
  ]
  h3_resolution: 9
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

library(here)

here("R", ".setup.R") |> source()
```

<!-- badges: start -->
[![Project Status: WIP – Initial development is in progress, but there has not yet been a stable, usable release suitable for the public.](https://www.repostatus.org/badges/latest/wip.svg)](https://www.repostatus.org/#wip)
[![](https://img.shields.io/badge/OSF-10.17605/OSF.IO/P5UFJ-1284C5.svg)](https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/P5UFJ)
[![License: GPLv3](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-GPLv3-bd0000.svg)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0)
[![License: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-CC_BY--NC--SA_4.0-lightgrey.svg)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/)
<!-- badges: end -->

## Overview

The present study aims to provide maps generated through the [Kernel Density Function](https://doc.arcgis.com/en/arcgis-online/analyze/kernel-density-global-function.htm) regarding access to healthy food in Brazilian favelas and urban communities by obtaining, processing, geocoding, and classifying [CNPJ](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CNPJ)s data from the Brazilian Federal Revenue Service ([RFB](https://www.gov.br/receitafederal/)), as well as acquiring, processing, geocoding, and classifying ([OSM](https://www.openstreetmap.org/#map=4/-15.13/-53.19)) establishment data via the [Overpass API](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Overpass_API). 

AThe classification of food healthfulness follows [Locais-Nova](https://doi.org/10.1590/S2237-96222025v34.20240361.en)study, using Group 0 (G0), which is not part of the original classification but rather a subgroup within G1 + G2. This subgroup is composed of establishments that exclusively sell healthy foods that should constitute part of the population’s diet.

For instructions on how to run this work, see the repository [README](https://github.com/cem-usp/kernel-accessibility/blob/main/README.md). 

## Problem

The [AcessoSAN](https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/ZE6WT) project aims to develop new methods for measuring and analyzing inequalities in access to healthy food, particularly in Brazilian favelas and urban communities. To achieve these objectives, it is necessary to use reliable and up-to-date datasets regarding establishments that sell healthy food.

The Brazilian Federal Revenue Service [RFB](https://www.gov.br/receitafederal/) rovides records of companies operating in Brazil, including food retailers. However, it does not provide [geocoded information](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Address_geocoding), which is necessary for spatial analysis. This study follows the piepelnes developed by @vartanian2026b pto georeference [RFB](https://www.gov.br/receitafederal/) [CNPJ](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CNPJ)s data and by @freitas2026o obtain accessibility metrics for Group G0.

Furthermore, in order to better understand the existing informational gaps regarding access to healthy food in favelas and urban communities, analyses will also be conducted using data available through the [Overpass API](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Overpass_API) as a means of comparison with the results obtained from the RFB. [RFB](https://www.gov.br/receitafederal/).

## Data Availability



## Methods

### Source of Data

Os dados usados nesse repositório advém das seguintes fontes:


- Receita Federal Brasileira ([RFB](https://www.gov.br/receitafederal/)):
  - Dados de pessoas jurídicas do Cadastro Nacional da Pessoa Jurídica ([CNPJ](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CNPJ)) [base de dados](https://dados.gov.br/dados/conjuntos-dados/cadastro-nacional-da-pessoa-juridica---cnpj) usados para identificar empresas [@brasil].
  - Códigos de agências e municípios brasileiros (_Tabela de Órgãos e Municípios_, [TOM]((https://dados.gov.br/dados/conjuntos-dados/tabela-de-rgos-e-municpios))) [@brasila], usados para identificar os municípios na base de dados dos CNPJs.
- Instituto Brasileiro de Geografia e Estatística ([IBGE](https://www.ibge.gov.br/)):
  - Códigos e descrições da Classificação Nacional de Atividade Econômicas ([CNAE](https://concla.ibge.gov.br/concla.html)) [@ibgej], usado para correspondência com os grupos Locais-Nova.
  - Dados dos municípios brasileiros via [`geobr`](https://ipeagit.github.io/geobr/), pacote R [@pereirab] usado para validação cruzada.
  - Base de dados espaciais aberta dos muninípios brasileiros disponível no Cadastro Nacional de Endereços para Fins Estatísticos ([CNEFE](https://www.ibge.gov.br/estatisticas/sociais/populacao/38734-cadastro-nacional-de-enderecos-para-fins-estatisticos.html)) via [`geocodebr`](https://ipeagit.github.io/geocodebr/), pacote R [@pereira] usado para geocodificação dos endereços.
  - Dados dos limites dos setores censitários provenientes do [Censo 2022](https://www.ibge.gov.br/en/statistics/social/labor/22836-2022-census-3.html) e daddos dos limites municipais por meio do [`geobr`](https://ipeagit.github.io/geobr/), pacote R [@pereirab] usado para análise espacial e cálculo da densidade populacional.
  - Dados populacionais do [Censo 2022](https://www.ibge.gov.br/en/statistics/social/labor/22836-2022-census-3.html) via [`censobr`](https://ipeagit.github.io/censobr/), pacote R [@pereirab], usado para calcular a densidade populacional.
- Centro de Estudos da Metrópole ([CEM](https://centrodametropole.fflch.usp.br/)):
  - [Tabela de Correspondência](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ipCw2FM3aUOdRd4w55J5SUEgXDCogxWZF-NZi0A-o_4/edit?usp=sharing) contendo a classificação que relaciona os códigos [CNAEs](https://www.gov.br/receitafederal/pt-br/assuntos/orientacao-tributaria/cadastros/cnpj/classificacao-nacional-de-atividades-economicas-2013-cnae) dos estabelecimentos aos grupos [Locais-Nova](https://doi.org/10.1590/S2237-96222025v34.20240361.en) (Fernandes & Giannotti, 2026).
  - [Tabela de Correspondência](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1JdDbKr5DYnQrp8DpI8Nep7v9UOpkruFkrKmo8LId8tQ/edit?usp=sharing) fornecendo padronização de endereços para fins de geocodificação (Vartanian et al., 2026).
- [Mapzen Terrain Tile](https://registry.opendata.aws/terrain-tiles/)
  - Dados altiimétrocos [@mapzen] via [`elevatr`](https://doi.org/10.32614/CRAN.package.elevatr), pacote R [@hollister2025] usado para cálculo do tempo de viagem e métricas de acessibilidade.
- OpenStreetMap ([OSM](https://www.openstreetmap.org/)):
  - Dados espaciais de [vias e caminhos para pedestres]((https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Map_features#Highway)) [@openstreetmap], obtidos por meio do projeto [Geofabrik](https://www.geofabrik.de/) [@geofabrik], utilizados para o cálculo dos tempos de deslocamento.
- [PMTilesBR](https://pmtiles.com.br): Dados Geoespaciais em Mosaico para o Brasil
  - Dados dos municípios brasileiros no formato [PMTiles](https://docs.protomaps.com/pmtiles/) [@vartanian2026c], utilizados para a representação de informações geográficas em mapas interativos com  [MapLibre](https://maplibre.org/) [@maplibre], via [`mapgl`](https://walker-data.com/mapgl/), pacote R [@walker2026a].
- [Tecnologia da Uber](https://www.uber.com/):
  - Dados de malhas hexagonais estruturados a partir do sistema hierárquico de indexação geoespacial hexagonal ([H3](https://h3geo.org/)) [@uber], processados com os pacotes [`h3r`](https://symbolixau.github.io/h3r) [@cooley2024] e [`h3jsr`](https://obrl-soil.github.io/h3jsr/) [@obrien2023], pacotes R empregados na criação de grades hexagonais para análise e visualização espacial.

## Data Munging

The data munging follows the data science workflow outlined by @wickham2023e, as illustrated in [@fig-wickham-at-al-2023-figure-1]. All processes were made using the [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) publishing system, along with the [AWK](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AWK) [@aho2023] and [R](https://www.r-project.org/) [@rcoreteam] programming languages, supported by several R packages.

For data manipulation and workflow, priority was given to packages from the [tidyverse](https://www.tidyverse.org/), [rOpenSci](https://ropensci.org/) and [r-spatial](https://r-spatial.org/) frameworks, as well as other packages adhering to the tidy tools manifesto [@wickham2023c].

::: {#fig-wickham-at-al-2023-figure-1}
![](images/wickham-at-al-2023-figure-1.png){width=75%}

[Source: Reproduced from @wickham2023e.]{.legend}

Data science workflow created by Wickham, Çetinkaya-Runde, and Grolemund.
:::

### Data Structure

#### Brazilian Federal Revenue Service (RFB)


#### Open Street Map (OSM)



### Travel Matrix

The travel matrix is computed using the [`r5r`](https://ipeagit.github.io/r5r/) R package [@pereira2021], which interfaces with the [R5](https://github.com/conveyal/r5) routing engine. This tool enables detailed travel time calculations based on multimodal transportation networks, accounting for road types, traffic conditions, and elevation changes. Travel times are derived from OpenStreetMap ([OSM](https://www.openstreetmap.org/)) road and footpath data [@openstreetmap] and elevation data from the Mapzen Terrain Tile dataset [@mapzen].

### Code Style

The Tidyverse [tidy tools manifesto](https://tidyverse.tidyverse.org/articles/manifesto.html) [@wickham2023c], [code style guide](https://style.tidyverse.org/) [@wickhama] and [design principles](https://design.tidyverse.org/) [@wickhamc] were followed to ensure consistency and enhance readability.

### Reproducibility

The pipeline is fully reproducible and can be run again at any time. To ensure consistent results, the [`renv`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/) package [@ushey2025] was used to manage and restore the R environment. See the [README](https://github.com/cem-usp/locais-nova-rfb-geocoding/blob/main/README.md) file in the code repository to learn how to run it.


### Load Packages

```{r}
#| code-fold: false
#| output: false
library(askpass)
library(brandr)
library(checkmate)
library(cli)
library(dplyr)
library(enderecobr)
library(fs)
library(geobr)
library(geocodebr)
library(ggplot2)
library(googlesheets4)
library(groomr) # github.com/danielvartan/groomr
library(here)
library(httr2)
library(labelled)
library(lobstr)
library(lockr) # github.com/danielvartan/lockr
library(lubridate)
library(magrittr)
library(nanoparquet)
library(numbersBR)
library(orbis) # github.com/danielvartan/orbis
library(osfr)
library(parallel)
library(purrr)
#library(pmtiles)
library(quarto)
library(readr)
library(scales)
library(sf)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(vroom)
library(xml2)
library(zip)
```

## Import, Process, Geocoded and Classify CNPJs from Bazilian Federal Revenue (RFB)

### Set the Environment

```{r}
#| label: Set Environment
#| include: false

library(cli)
library(httr2)

if (!is_online()) {
  cli_abort(
    paste0(
      "No {.strong {col_red('internet connection')}} detected. ",
      "Please check your connection and try again."
    )
  )
}
```

#### Set Keys

::: {.callout-note}
Veja a seção [Disponibilidade dos Dados](#disponibilidade-dos-dados) para mais informações.
:::

```{r}
#| output: false
#| echo: true

osf_auth(Sys.getenv("OSF_PAT")) # askpass())
```

```{r}
#| output: false
#| echo: true

gs4_auth(
  cache = here(".secrets"),
  email = TRUE,
  use_oob = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

public_key <- here(".ssh", "id_rsa.pub")
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

private_key <- here(".ssh", "id_rsa")
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

password <- Sys.getenv("ACESSOSAN_PASSWORD") # askpass()
```

#### Set Data Directories

```{r}
#| echo: true

raw_data_dir <- here("data-raw")
input_data_dir <- here("data-input")
data_dir <- here("data")
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

for (i in c(raw_data_dir, input_data_dir, data_dir)) {
  if (!dir_exists(i)) {
    dir_create(i, recurse = TRUE)
  }
}
```

#### Check Parameters

::: {.callout-note}
The pipeline main parameters are defined in the [YAML](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/YAML) header of its [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) document.

The `year` and `month` variables represent the year and month of the consolidated [RFB](https://www.gov.br/receitafederal/) CNPJ dataset.

The `municipality_codes` variable identifies the [IBGE](https://www.ibge.gov.br/) codes of the municipalities included in the analysis. Click [here](https://sidra.ibge.gov.br/territorio) to see a list of all Brazilian municipalities and their codes.
:::

```{r}
#| include: false
#| echo: true

if (!exists("params")) {
  params <-
    here("index.qmd") |>
    quarto_inspect() |>
    pluck("formats", "html", "metadata", "params")
}
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

params |> lobstr::tree()
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

assert_int(params$year, lower = 1980, upper = 2050)
assert_int(params$month, lower = 1, upper = 12)
assert_integerish(params$municipality_codes, lower = 1100015, upper = 5300108)
```

### Download and Import Locais-Nova CNAEs Lookup Table

::: {.callout-note}
See the [Source of Data](#source-of-data) section for more information.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
Although *restaurants* are part of the [Locais-Nova](https://doi.org/10.1590/S2237-96222025v34.20240361.pt) classification, they were excluded from this analysis due to conflicting evidence on how to reliably identify them using company registry data.
:::

#### Download Data

```{r}
#| label: Download and Import Locais-Nova CNAE Lookup Table
#| echo: true

locais_nova_lookup_file <-
  input_data_dir |>
  file.path("locais-nova-lookup-data.rds")
```

```{r}
#| eval: !expr '!file_exists(locais_nova_lookup_file)'
#| output: false
#| echo: true

if (!file_exists(locais_nova_lookup_file)) {
  locais_nova_lookup_data <-
    "1ipCw2FM3aUOdRd4w55J5SUEgXDCogxWZF-NZi0A-o_4" |>
    read_sheet(sheet = "Dataset")

  locais_nova_lookup_data |> write_rds(locais_nova_lookup_file)
}
```

#### Import Data

```{r}
#| echo: true

locais_nova_lookup_data <- locais_nova_lookup_file |> read_rds()
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

locais_nova_lookup_data |> glimpse()
```

### Download, Import, and Process Municipality Data

::: {.callout-note}
See the [Source of Data](#source-of-data) section for more information.
:::

#### Download and Save Data

```{r}
#| label: Download, Import, and Process Municipality Data
#| echo: true

read_municipality_file <-
  input_data_dir |>
  file.path(
    paste0(
      paste(
        "read_municipality",
        "code",
        "all",
        "year",
        params$year |> closest_geobr_year(type = "municipality"),
        "simplified",
        FALSE,
        sep = "-"
      ),
      ".rds"
    )
  )
```

```{r}
#| eval: !expr '!file_exists(read_municipality_file)'
#| output: false
#| echo: true

if (!file_exists(read_municipality_file)) {
  municipalities_data <-
    read_municipality(
      year = params$year |> closest_geobr_year(type = "municipality"),
      simplified = FALSE,
      showProgress = FALSE
    )

  municipalities_data |> write_rds(read_municipality_file)
}
```

#### Import Data

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_data <- read_municipality_file |> read_rds()
```

```{r}
#| include: false

if (is.null(municipalities_data)) {
  file_delete(read_municipality_file)

  cli_abort(
    paste0(
      "Failed to download {.strong {col_red('municipalities data')}}. ",
      "This can be caused by a temporary issue with the {.code geobr} ",
      "package. Check your internet connection and try again."
    )
  )
}
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_data |> glimpse()
```

#### Filter Data

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_data <-
  municipalities_data |>
  filter(code_muni %in% params$municipality_codes)
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_data |> glimpse()
```

#### Transform Data

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_data <-
  municipalities_data |>
  st_transform(st_crs(4326)) |>
  select(
    municipality = name_muni,
    municipality_code = code_muni,
    state = name_state,
    state_code = code_state,
    federal_unit = abbrev_state,
    region = name_region,
    region_code = code_region,
    geometry = geom
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_data |> glimpse()
```

### Download, Import, and Transform Agencies and Municipalities (TOM) Data

::: {.callout-note}
See the [Source of Data](#source-of-data) section for more information.
:::

#### Download Data

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_tom_data_file <-
  input_data_dir |>
  file.path("municipalities_tom_data.csv")
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

if (!file_exists(municipalities_tom_data_file)) {
  request("https://www.gov.br") |>
    req_url_path_append("receitafederal") |>
    req_url_path_append("dados") |>
    req_url_path_append("municipios.csv") |>
    req_user_agent("https://cem-usp.github.io/kernel-accessibility") |>
    req_options(connecttimeout = 10) |>
    req_retry(
      max_tries = 3,
      retry_on_failure = TRUE,
      backoff = \(attempt) 5^attempt
    ) |>
    req_progress() |>
    req_perform(path = municipalities_tom_data_file)
}
```

#### Importa Data

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_tom_data <-
  municipalities_tom_data_file |>
  read_delim(
    delim = ";",
    col_names = FALSE,
    col_types = cols(.default = "c"),
    progress = FALSE
  ) |>
  slice(-1)
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_tom_data |> glimpse()
```

#### Transform Data

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_tom_data <-
  municipalities_tom_data |>
  mutate(
    across(
      .cols = everything(),
      .fns = \(x) iconv(x, from = "Windows-1252", to = "UTF-8") # iconvlist()
    )
  ) |>
  set_names(
    c(
      "municipality_code_tom",
      "municipality_code_ibge",
      "municipality_tom",
      "municipality_ibge",
      "federal_unit"
    )
  ) |>
  mutate(
    across(
      .cols = starts_with("municipality_code"),
      .fns = as.integer
    )
  ) |>
  relocate(
    municipality_tom,
    municipality_code_tom,
    municipality_ibge,
    municipality_code_ibge,
    federal_unit
  )
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipalities_tom_data |> glimpse()
```

### Map IBGE Municipality Codes to TOM Codes

::: {.callout-note}
The Brazilian Federal Revenue Service ([RFB](https://www.gov.br/receitafederal/)) identifies municipalities using a different coding scheme than IBGE codes (_Tabela de Órgãos e Municípios_, [TOM](https://dados.gov.br/dados/conjuntos-dados/tabela-de-rgos-e-municpios)). Mapping IBGE codes to the corresponding TOM codes is therefore required.
:::

```{r}
#| label: Map IBGE Municipality Codes to TOM Codes
#| echo: true

params$municipality_codes
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipality_tom_codes <-
  municipalities_tom_data |>
  filter(municipality_code_ibge %in% params$municipality_codes) |>
  pull(municipality_code_tom) |>
  str_pad(width = 4, pad = "0")
```

```{r}
#| echo: true

municipality_tom_codes
```

### Download RFB Data

::: {.callout-note}
See the [Source of Data](#source-of-data) section for more information.
:::

#### List Files

```{r}
#| label: Download RFB Data

public_token <- "YggdBLfdninEJX9"
```

```{r}
request <-
  request("https://arquivos.receitafederal.gov.br") |>
  req_auth_basic(public_token, "")
```

```{r}
response <-
  request |>
  req_url_path_append("public.php") |>
  req_url_path_append("webdav") |>
  req_method("PROPFIND") |>
  req_url_path_append(
    paste0(params$year, "-", str_pad(params$month, 2, pad = 0))
  ) |>
  req_headers("Depth" = "1") |>
  req_body_raw(
    '<?xml version="1.0"?>
      <d:propfind xmlns:d="DAV:">
        <d:prop>
          <d:displayname/>
          <d:getcontentlength/>
          <d:getcontenttype/>
          <d:getlastmodified/>
        </d:prop>
      </d:propfind>',
    type = "application/xml"
  ) |>
  req_user_agent("https://cem-usp.github.io/kernel-accessibility") |>
  req_options(connecttimeout = 10) |>
  req_retry(
    max_tries = 3,
    retry_on_failure = TRUE,
    backoff = \(attempt) 5^attempt
  ) |>
  req_perform() |>
  resp_body_xml() %>%
  xml_find_all(
    .,
    ".//d:response",
    xml_ns(.)
  )
```

```{r}
list_files <- function(node, response) {
  xpaths <- list(
    name = ".//d:displayname",
    url = ".//d:href",
    size = ".//d:getcontentlength",
    type = ".//d:getcontenttype",
    modified = ".//d:getlastmodified"
  )

  xpaths |>
    map(
      function(xpath) {
        node |>
          xml_find_first(xpath, xml_ns(response)) |>
          xml_text()
      }
    ) |>
    as_tibble()
}
```

```{r}
files <-
  response |>
  map(list_files, response = response) |>
  list_rbind() |>
  filter(type == "application/zip") |>
  mutate(size = size |> as_fs_bytes())
```

```{r}
files |> glimpse()
```

#### Download Data

```{r}
rfb_file_pattern <-
  paste0(
    "D",
    params$year |> str_sub(-1),
    params$month |> str_pad(width = 2, pad = "0")
  )
```

```{r}
#| eval: !expr '!all(str_detect(dir_ls(raw_data_dir), rfb_file_pattern))'

if (
  raw_data_dir |>
    dir_ls() |>
    str_detect(rfb_file_pattern) |>
    all() |>
    not()
) {
  cli_progress_bar(
    "Downloading RFB files",
    total = nrow(files),
    clear = FALSE
  )

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(files))) {
    request |>
      req_url_path_append(files$url[i]) |>
      req_perform(
        path = here(raw_data_dir, files$name[i])
      )

    cli_progress_update()
  }

  cli_progress_done()
}
```

#### Unzip Files

```{r}
zip_files <-
  raw_data_dir |>
  dir_ls(type = "file", regexp = "\\.zip$")
```

```{r}
#| eval: !expr '!all(str_detect(dir_ls(raw_data_dir), rfb_file_pattern))'
#| output: false

if (length(zip_files) > 0) {
  zip_files |>
    map(
      \(x) unzip(x, exdir = raw_data_dir),
      .progress = TRUE
    )
}
```

```{r}
#| eval: !expr '!all(str_detect(dir_ls(raw_data_dir), rfb_file_pattern))'

if (length(zip_files) > 0) {
  zip_files |> file_delete()
}
```

### Import and Filter Data

#### Import and Filter Active and Regular Establishments by Municipalities

::: {.callout-note}
The [AWK](https://www.gnu.org/software/gawk/) programming language and the [vroom](https://vroom.r-lib.org/) R package are used to read and filter the data efficiently, without loading it entirely into memory.

Only establishments with an *Active* registration status (`$6 == "02"`) and no special status (`$29 == ""`) are included, and the resulting records are further filtered by municipality (`$21 == municipality_tom_codes`).

Click [here](https://dados.gov.br/dados/conjuntos-dados/cadastro-nacional-da-pessoa-juridica---cnpj) to access the data dictionary.
:::

```{r}
#| label: Import and Filter Data

data <-
  raw_data_dir |>
  dir_ls(
    type = "file",
    regexp = "\\.ESTABELE$"
  ) |>
  map(
    function(x) {
      vroom(
        file = pipe(
          paste0(
            "awk ",
            "-F ",
            "';' ",
            "'{ ",
            "if (",
            '($6 == "',
            '\\"02\\"',
            '")',
            " && (",
            paste0(
              '$21 == "\\"',
              municipality_tom_codes,
              '\\""',
              collapse = " || "
            ),
            ") && ",
            '($29 == "',
            '\\"\\"',
            '")',
            ") ",
            "print",
            " }' ",
            str_escape(x)
          )
        ),
        delim = ";",
        col_names = c(
          "cnpj_basic",
          "cnpj_order",
          "cnpj_dv",
          "branch_identifier",
          "trade_name",
          "registration_status",
          "registration_status_date",
          "registration_status_reason",
          "foreign_city_name",
          "country",
          "opening_date",
          "primary_cnae",
          "secondary_cnae",
          "street_type",
          "street_name",
          "number",
          "complement",
          "neighborhood",
          "postal_code",
          "federal_unit",
          "municipality_code_tom",
          "phone_area_code_1",
          "phone_number_1",
          "phone_area_code_2",
          "phone_number_2",
          "fax_area_code",
          "fax_number",
          "email",
          "special_status",
          "special_status_date"
        ),
        col_types = cols(.default = "c"),
        col_select = c(
          "cnpj_basic",
          "cnpj_order",
          "cnpj_dv",
          "trade_name",
          "opening_date",
          "primary_cnae",
          "secondary_cnae",
          "street_type",
          "street_name",
          "number",
          "complement",
          "neighborhood",
          "postal_code",
          "municipality_code_tom",
          "federal_unit",
          "registration_status_date"
        ),
        guess_max = 100,
        num_threads = detectCores() |>
          multiply_by(0.75) |>
          ceiling(),
        progress = TRUE,
        show_col_types = FALSE
      )
    },
    .progress = TRUE
  ) |>
  list_rbind()
```

```{r}
data |> glimpse()
```

#### Filter Establishments by Locais-Nova G0 Group

#### Import and Filter Additional Company Data by Establishment

#### Import and Filter Simples Nacional Data by Establishment

#### Visualize Registration Status Dates

::: {.callout-note}
These dates indicate when establishments were registered as *Active*.
:::

### Tidy Data

::: {.callout-note}
Some tidying operations are performed to ensure the data is in a suitable format for analysis. The data is not yet reshaped to a [tidy format](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/data-tidy.html) to avoid duplicated cases during geocoding.

Addresses were normalized using the [`enderecobr`](https://ipeagit.github.io/enderecobr/) R package [@herszenhut], which standardizes common street types and abbreviations in Brazilian addresses.
:::

#### Remove Extra Columns

#### Standardize Character Encoding

#### Pad CNPJs and CNAEs

#### Remove CNPJ Duplicates

#### Remove Invalid CNPJs and CNAEs

#### Add Municipality Data

#### Standardize Data

#### Relocate Columns

#### Arrange Data

### Geocode Data

#### Prepare Data

#### Geocode Data

#### Tidy Data

#### Visualize Geocoding Precision

##### Global Precision

##### Local Precision

### Tidy Data

#### Remove Extra Columns

####  Pivot Data

#### Arrange Data

### Create Data Dictionary

#### Prepare Metadata

#### Visualize Final Data

### Save Data

::: {.callout-note}
The processed data are available in [`csv`](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comma-separated_values), [`rds`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/readRDS.html) and [`parquet`](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apache_Parquet) formats through a dedicated repository on the Open Science Framework ([OSF](https://osf.io/)). See the [Data Availability](#data-availability) section for more information.
:::

#### Clean Old Data Files

#### Write Data 

#### Write Lookup Tables

#### Lock Data

#### Write Meetadata

### Visualize Geocoding

::: {.callout-note}
The processed data can be visualized at [RFB and OSM Georeferenced Data](#rfb-and-osm-georeferenced-data).
:::

## Open Street Map (OSM) Establishment Data Acquisition, Processing, Geocoding, and Classification



## Citation

::: {.callout-note}
When using this data, you must also cite the original data sources.
:::

## License

::: {style="text-align: left;"}
[![License: GPLv3](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-GPLv3-bd0000.svg)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0)
[![License: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-CC_BY--NC--SA_4.0-lightgrey.svg)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/)
:::

::: {.callout-note}
The original data sources may be subject to their own licensing terms and conditions.
:::

The code in this repository is licensed under the [GNU General Public License Version 3](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0), while the report is available under the [Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/) license.


```
Copyright (C) 2026 Center for Metropolitan Studies

The code in this report is free software: you can redistribute it and/or
modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the
Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or (at your option)
any later version.

This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY
WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A
PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.

You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
this program. If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
```

## Reconhecimentos

:::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; margin: 1em; padding-top: 1em;"}
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: center;"}
[![](images/acessosan-logo.svg){width="140"}](https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/ZE6WT)
:::
::: {.column style="width: 70%; display: flex; align-items: center;"}
This work is part of a research project by the Polytechnic School ([Poli](https://www.poli.usp.br/)) of the University of São Paulo ([USP](https://usp.br/)), in partnership with the National Secretariat for Food and Nutrition Security ([SESAN](https://www.gov.br/mds/pt-br/orgaos/SESAN)) of the Brazilian Ministry of Social Development and Assistance, Family and Fight Against Hunger ([MDS](https://www.gov.br/mds/)), titled: *AcessoSAN: Mapping Food Access to Support Public Policies on Food and Nutrition Security and Hunger Reduction in Brazilian Cities*.
:::
::::

:::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; margin: 1em; padding-top: 0em;"}
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: center;"}
[![](images/cem-icon.svg){width="190"}](https://centrodametropole.fflch.usp.br)
:::
::: {.column style="width: 70%; display: flex; align-items: center;"}
This work was developed with support from the Center for Metropolitan Studies ([CEM](https://centrodametropole.fflch.usp.br)) based at the School of Philosophy, Letters and Human Sciences ([FFLCH](https://www.fflch.usp.br/)) of the University of São Paulo ([USP](https://usp.br/)) and at the Brazilian Center for Analysis and Planning ([CEBRAP](https://cebrap.org.br/)).
:::
::::

:::: {.columns style="display: flex; align-items: center; margin: 1em; padding-top: 0em;"}
::: {.column style="width: 30%; text-align: center;"}
[![](images/fapesp-logo.svg){width="160"}](https://fapesp.br/)
:::
::: {.column style="width: 70%; display: flex; align-items: center;"}
This study was financed, in part, by the São Paulo Research Foundation ([FAPESP](https://fapesp.br/)), Brazil. Process Number [2025/17879-2](https://bv.fapesp.br/en/bolsas/231507/geospatial-data-science-applied-to-food-policies/).
:::
::::

## Referencias {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
